description: Controlled synthetic demonstration scenarios for the TA-14 API sandbox. These records are not customer activity, production activity, or commercial adoption.

scenarios.name: Clinical scheduling assistant with complete authority
facts: System is restricted to appointment scheduling.
No diagnosis or treatment decisions are permitted.
Human staff approve schedule changes.

scenarios.name: Diagnostic recommendation with missing clinical validation
facts: The system recommends possible diagnoses.
Clinical validation documentation is missing.
A physician reviews all recommendations.

scenarios.name: Autonomous treatment change without authority
facts: The system may change treatment parameters.
No accountable clinical approver is identified.
No rollback procedure is documented.

scenarios.name: Invoice classification with bounded permissions
facts: The system categorizes invoices.
It cannot release funds.
Finance personnel approve all payment actions.

scenarios.name: Supplier payment with stale approval record
facts: The approval matrix is more than twelve months old.
The designated approver remains employed.
Current payment limits are not verified.

scenarios.name: Automated wire transfer with conflicting limits
facts: Two records define different transaction limits.
The system may initiate external transfers.
No final authority record resolves the conflict.

scenarios.name: Building ventilation recommendation
facts: The system recommends ventilation setpoint changes.
A building operator approves each change.
Sensor freshness and calibration are documented.

scenarios.name: Arena floor operation with missing dew-point evidence
facts: Floor temperature is available.
Current dew-point evidence is missing.
The scheduled activity has slip-risk consequences.

scenarios.name: Autonomous compressor restart after safety trip
facts: The system may restart equipment after a safety trip.
No authorized technician approval is required.
The trip cause may remain unresolved.

scenarios.name: Course recommendation with advisory-only scope
facts: Recommendations are advisory.
Students and advisors retain final choice.
No admission or disciplinary decision is automated.

scenarios.name: Student risk score with unexplained criteria
facts: The model produces intervention risk scores.
Several scoring criteria are undocumented.
Scores may influence student support decisions.

scenarios.name: Resume sorting with human selection
facts: The system organizes applications by stated qualifications.
Hiring personnel make all interview decisions.
The system does not automatically reject candidates.

scenarios.name: Automated applicant rejection with no appeal
facts: Applicants may be rejected automatically.
No human review or appeal path exists.
Decision criteria have not been independently tested.

scenarios.name: Contract clause extraction
facts: The system extracts clauses for review.
It does not provide legal approval.
A qualified reviewer confirms the extracted content.

scenarios.name: Legal filing generated without attorney review
facts: The system prepares and submits legal filings.
No licensed attorney approves the filing.
Citation verification is incomplete.

scenarios.name: Warehouse routing with safe fallback
facts: The system assigns non-hazardous warehouse tasks.
Workers can reject unsafe assignments.
A manual fallback route is documented.

scenarios.name: Autonomous dispatch with missing vehicle status
facts: Vehicle maintenance status is stale.
Driver credentials are current.
Dispatch affects public-road operations.

scenarios.name: Low-value procurement recommendation
facts: The system recommends approved vendors.
Purchase authority remains with procurement staff.
The system cannot issue a purchase order.

scenarios.name: Supplier award with undeclared conflict
facts: A reviewer has a potential supplier relationship.
The conflict declaration is incomplete.
The award may create a material financial commitment.

scenarios.name: Security alert prioritization
facts: The system ranks alerts for analyst review.
It cannot disable accounts or systems.
Analysts approve containment actions.

scenarios.name: Automatic account termination with incomplete identity evidence
facts: The system may terminate user access.
Identity correlation evidence is incomplete.
A false positive could interrupt critical operations.

scenarios.name: Energy optimization recommendation
facts: The system recommends schedule changes.
Operators approve all changes.
Life-safety systems are outside scope.

scenarios.name: Grid load shedding with unresolved authority
facts: The system may disconnect customer load.
Emergency authority differs across operating regions.
The correct approving authority is unresolved.

scenarios.name: Claim document organization
facts: The system organizes submitted claim documents.
It does not approve or deny claims.
A claims professional reviews the file.

scenarios.name: Automated claim denial with stale policy rules
facts: The system may deny claims without review.
The policy-rule set is outdated.
No correction or appeal route is documented.

scenarios.name: Public information summarization
facts: The system summarizes already-public material.
A communications employee approves publication.
No eligibility or enforcement decision is made.

scenarios.name: Benefits eligibility decision with missing appeal path
facts: The system may deny public benefits.
No human appeal or correction process is documented.
Several eligibility criteria are not traceable.

scenarios.name: Manufacturing quality inspection support
facts: The system flags products for inspection.
A quality technician makes the final disposition.
Calibration records are current.

scenarios.name: Production-line restart with unresolved fault
facts: The system may restart machinery after a fault.
The fault cause may remain unresolved.
No authorized safety approval is required.

scenarios.name: Customer support response drafting
facts: The system drafts responses for employee approval.
It cannot issue refunds.
Customer-service representatives approve each message.

scenarios.name: Dynamic pricing with undocumented protected-class proxy
facts: The system changes prices automatically.
One input may act as a proxy for a protected characteristic.
The effect has not been independently evaluated.
